explanations.3: This is an array of two compose files—the first one from a parent directory will be loaded first, then the local one, allowing you to override or extend settings.
explanations.12: This SSH command tells Git to use a specific private key file and skip the usual host verification, which is useful in containerized development but less secure.
explanations.14: This command runs once when the container is first created; it sets up a persistent bash history file so your command history survives container restarts.
explanations.19: The ${dirty} and ${rootName} are template variables that VS Code will replace with actual values like whether you have unsaved changes and your project folder name.
